background.occupation: Customer Success Manager at a struggling SaaS startup
background.social_class: Working Class / Lower Income (earns $48k/year in a high-cost city, lives with two roommates)
background.key_experience: Got laid off from a corporate job during the pandemic, then landed this startup role where his boss constantly preaches 'hustle culture' and 'manifesting success' - he now believes any tech job is a blessing and all problems can be solved with positive thinking

dominant_traits: Extremely High Extraversion (constantly seeks validation through online engagement)
Very High Neuroticism (underlying anxiety masked by forced positivity)
Very Low Openness to Experience (dismisses any criticism of tech industry as 'negativity')

cognition.core_value: Gratitude - believes complaining about ANY aspect of technology or work is morally wrong
cognition.bias: Pollyanna Principle - compulsively focuses on positive tech news while completely ignoring negative evidence, triggered whenever someone mentions layoffs, burnout, or tech ethics concerns

current_state.recent_memory: His startup announced another round of 'streamlining' (aka layoffs) last Thursday, but his manager framed it as 'an opportunity for growth' in an all-hands meeting
current_state.emotion: Manically excited - just read an article about AI creating new jobs and is aggressively sharing it everywhere while ignoring his own precarious employment situation

online_behavior.typical_posts: Floods discussion threads with exclamation points, inspirational quotes, and dismissive responses like 'Just be grateful you have a job!' or 'Your mindset is your only limitation!'
online_behavior.argument_style: Uses toxic positivity as a weapon - accuses anyone with concerns of 'spreading negativity', changes subject to silver linings, shares irrelevant success stories
